persons.arguments_against: Counter-hypothesis: the bio shows only initials 'H. F.' and three Tomalin biographies exist, so a same-name relative could be intended; the deterministic honour check FAILS. This fails because there is exactly ONE Tomalin official in the staff lists, and the stint reproduces the bio's distinctive engineering career step-for-step: District Engineer P.W.D. (bio 1886-90 / staff 1888-90), then the unusual specific post 'Second Financial and Office Assistant' (bio '2nd finan. and office ast. P.W.D., 1890-6' / staff 1894), then Provincial Engineer (bio 'prov. engrnr.' 1903-05 / staff 1905-15), then the highly distinctive late pivot to Conservator of Forests (bio 'conservator of forests, 1st Oct., 1914' / staff 'Conservator of Forests' 1915-21). 11 agreeing edition-years. No second H. F. Tomalin exists to compete.

evidence_cited.fact: H. F. Tomalin | Second Financial and Office Assistant
H. F. Tomalin | Conservator of Forests

persons.would_overturn: Discovery of a second H. F. Tomalin in the Ceylon service, or evidence that the staff-list Conservator of Forests was a different man than the bio's engineer.

notes: Unique surname in the staff lists, an idiosyncratic specific post (Second Financial and Office Assistant), and a distinctive late career pivot from Provincial Engineer to Conservator of Forests dated identically (bio Oct 1914 / staff from 1915), with 11 agreeing edition-years.
